analysis_population.exclusion_rule: reuse the frozen Record160 excluded_no_successful_source rule exactly; no post-hoc exclusion is allowed

current_policy.name: original_index_v1
current_policy.tie_definition: all primary key values compare exactly equal after the existing production score rounding and field normalization; no tolerance, bucket, or approximate comparison is permitted

deterministic_tiebreak_v2.name: deterministic_tiebreak_v2
stable_key_precedence: sorted exact stable identifiers from unified IdentityProfile
exact normalized title plus sorted normalized authors plus year when all are present
sorted registered source names plus sorted registered source record references plus normalized field-summary SHA-256

validation_invariants: the frozen current_rules candidate pool, scores, categories, identities, events, lineage, reranked output, and final output reconstruct exactly
an exact tie is formed only when every registered production primary sort-key value compares exactly equal
deterministic_tiebreak_v2 uses no input position or execution-order information
every v2 fallback key binds registered source record references and a normalized field-summary digest
v2 produces the same ranked semantic document under every preregistered permutation
score, category, deduplicated identity set, field lineage, semantic events, and non-tie relative order remain unchanged
any Top-20 membership change, non-tie order change, or authoritative identity change yields not_qualified
only exact-tie internal order may differ for qualified_for_review
the current production default remains original_index_v1
snapshot tree remains unchanged and no network, LLM, evaluator, or quality path is used
warnings: This qualification addresses deterministic ordering only; it does not establish relevance, Precision, Recall, F1, or an official score.
qualified_for_review does not enable deterministic_tiebreak_v2 or change current_rules evidence conclusions.
Permutation profiles reorder one immutable deduplicated candidate pool; they do not re-run or alter production identity merging.
